note: V1 acceptance criteria are AC-001 through AC-012. V1.5 uses AC-101 through AC-150 to avoid collision.

acceptance_criteria.description: Backend API is containerized (Dockerfile present) and builds successfully into a Docker image < 200 MB.
verify: cmd: docker build -t couplesync-api ./backend && docker image inspect couplesync-api --format='{{.Size}}'
manual: confirm image size is under 200 MB

acceptance_criteria.description: Backend deploys to Azure Container Apps Consumption Plan and is reachable at a public HTTPS URL.
verify: manual: deploy via GitHub Actions workflow and confirm the revision is running in Azure portal
cmd: curl -s https://<app>.azurecontainerapps.io/api/v1/health | jq .status

acceptance_criteria.description: EF Core migrations run on startup against Neon.tech PostgreSQL; all existing schema objects are created correctly.
verify: manual: deploy fresh container with empty Neon database and confirm migrations complete without error
manual: verify all V1 tables (users, couples, transactions, goals, etc.) exist in Neon via psql

acceptance_criteria.description: All runtime secrets (DATABASE_URL, JWT_SECRET, FCM credentials, OCR keys, GEMINI_API_KEY) are stored as Azure Container Apps secrets or environment variables and never committed to the repository.
verify: manual: CI secret scan (git-secrets or trufflehog) passes with no findings on main branch
manual: confirm each secret is present in Azure Container Apps configuration and absent from all source files

acceptance_criteria.description: EAS Build with preview profile (buildType: apk) produces a valid installable APK artifact.
verify: cmd: eas build --platform android --profile preview --non-interactive
manual: download and install APK on a physical Android device

acceptance_criteria.description: GitHub Actions workflow uploads the APK to Firebase App Distribution and notifies the configured pilot tester group.
verify: manual: trigger CI on main; confirm APK appears in Firebase App Distribution console with correct tester group
manual: tester receives email invite and can install APK

acceptance_criteria.description: Health endpoint (GET /api/v1/health) returns HTTP 200 with {status: 'healthy'} from the cloud deployment.
verify: cmd: curl -f https://<app>.azurecontainerapps.io/api/v1/health

acceptance_criteria.description: GitHub Actions CI pipeline runs dotnet test and Expo type checks; deployment is blocked if any step fails.
verify: manual: introduce a failing test and confirm deployment step is skipped
cmd: dotnet test --no-build (must exit 0 on main branch)

acceptance_criteria.description: Existing 219 tests continue to pass against the Neon.tech PostgreSQL connection string (integration tests use test DB).
verify: cmd: dotnet test --no-build (green, 219+ passing)
manual: integration tests configured to point at test Neon DB or local SQLite in CI

acceptance_criteria.description: All cloud services used are on 100% free tier; no paid tier is required for pilot operation.
verify: manual: Azure portal shows Consumption Plan with no cost commitment
manual: Neon dashboard shows free tier project
manual: Firebase / Google Cloud console shows no billing charges

acceptance_criteria.description: A couple can set their monthly gross income via PUT/POST /api/v1/budgets and retrieve it in GET /api/v1/budgets/current.
verify: cmd: integration test — POST /api/v1/budgets with gross_income=10000, GET /api/v1/budgets/current returns gross_income=10000
manual: UI — Budget screen shows gross income field editable by either partner

acceptance_criteria.description: A couple can create budget allocations per category (e.g., Food=2000, Transport=500) and persist them.
verify: cmd: integration test — PUT /api/v1/budgets/{id}/allocations with category list, GET returns same list
manual: UI — Budget screen lists categories with allocated amounts

acceptance_criteria.description: GET /api/v1/budgets/current returns per-category actual spending computed from current-month transactions alongside allocated amounts.
verify: cmd: integration test — seed 3 transactions in current month, GET /api/v1/budgets/current shows correct actual_spent per matching category
manual: dashboard/budget screen shows 'Spent R$X of R$Y' per category

acceptance_criteria.description: Both partners see identical budget plan and spending data when querying GET /api/v1/budgets/current (couple-scoped).
verify: manual: login as Partner A and Partner B, both GET /api/v1/budgets/current return same JSON payload

acceptance_criteria.description: An FCM alert of type BUDGET_EXCEEDED is triggered when actual spending exceeds the allocated amount for a category.
verify: manual: seed transactions that exceed category budget, confirm FCM message delivered with type BUDGET_EXCEEDED

acceptance_criteria.description: GET /api/v1/budgets/current includes budget_gap = gross_income minus sum of all allocated_amount.
verify: cmd: integration test — verify budget_gap field value matches arithmetic

acceptance_criteria.description: Budget endpoints return HTTP 403 when accessed by a user from a different couple.
verify: cmd: integration test — user from couple B attempts GET /api/v1/budgets/current scoped to couple A; expect 403

acceptance_criteria.description: Budget plan is keyed per calendar month (YYYY-MM); creating a budget in a new month does not overwrite the previous month's plan.
verify: cmd: integration test — create budget for 2026-04, create budget for 2026-05, GET for each month returns independent plans

acceptance_criteria.description: Budget management UI is accessible from the mobile app within 3 taps from the main navigation.
verify: manual: tap count walkthrough — bottom nav → Budget tab → Budget screen (2 taps from any screen)

acceptance_criteria.description: Budget endpoints have integration tests covering create, update, read, couple isolation, and overspend detection.
verify: cmd: dotnet test --filter Category=Budget (all budget integration tests green)

acceptance_criteria.description: Mobile app provides camera capture and file-picker (image or PDF) on the Transactions screen for OCR import.
verify: manual: open Transactions screen, tap import button, confirm camera and file picker options appear

acceptance_criteria.description: File upload via POST /api/v1/ocr/upload stores the file securely in cloud storage with couple-scoped path and returns upload_id.
verify: cmd: integration test — POST a JPEG and a PDF; confirm upload_id returned and file exists in storage under /{couple_id}/{upload_id}/
manual: verify no cross-couple path access possible via path traversal check

acceptance_criteria.description: Backend triggers OCR processing and GET /api/v1/ocr/{upload_id}/results returns extracted transaction candidates with date, description, amount, currency, and confidence.
verify: manual: upload a sample bank statement JPEG; confirm results contain at least one transaction candidate with all required fields
manual: confidence values are between 0 and 1

acceptance_criteria.description: Mobile displays a diff/merge review screen listing extracted candidates with checkboxes and editable description and amount fields.
verify: manual: complete an OCR upload cycle; confirm review screen appears with selectable, editable rows

acceptance_criteria.description: POST /api/v1/ocr/{upload_id}/confirm with selected_indices creates only the selected transactions and skips unselected ones.
verify: cmd: integration test — upload, extract 3 candidates, confirm indices [0, 2] only; verify 2 transactions created

acceptance_criteria.description: Duplicate fingerprint detection flags candidates that match existing transactions; duplicates are shown with a warning but user can still import.
verify: cmd: integration test — import a transaction, re-upload same statement, verify duplicate_suspected=true on matching candidate
manual: confirm user can still check and import the flagged candidate

acceptance_criteria.description: Confirmed imported transactions appear in the shared transaction list and are included in dashboard aggregations.
verify: manual: complete OCR import, open Transactions list, confirm imported items appear; check dashboard totals updated

acceptance_criteria.description: All OCR endpoints return HTTP 403 for requests with a couple_id that does not own the upload_id.
verify: cmd: integration test — couple B attempts GET /api/v1/ocr/{upload_id_of_couple_A}/results; expect 403

acceptance_criteria.description: GET /api/v1/ocr/{upload_id}/status returns correct state (PENDING, PROCESSING, READY, FAILED) during OCR lifecycle.
verify: cmd: integration test with mocked OCR service — verify all four status transitions reachable
manual: mobile polls status and shows progress indicator until READY or FAILED

acceptance_criteria.description: When Azure AI Document Intelligence quota is exhausted, POST /api/v1/ocr/upload returns HTTP 429 with error code quota_exhausted and a reset date; mobile surfaces recovery guidance.
verify: cmd: integration test with mocked quota error — confirm 429 response shape
manual: mobile shows 'OCR unavailable this month, quota reached. Try again on [date]'

acceptance_criteria.description: mobile/src/theme/index.ts exists and exports colors, spacing, typography, borderRadius, and shadows tokens.
verify: cmd: npx tsc --noEmit (no TypeScript errors in theme module)
manual: inspect theme file exports match all five token groups

acceptance_criteria.description: No hardcoded hex color values exist in .tsx files outside mobile/src/theme/index.ts.
verify: cmd: grep -rn '#[0-9A-Fa-f]{3,6}' mobile/src/modules mobile/app --include='*.tsx' (zero results)

acceptance_criteria.description: A reusable LoadingState component is used on all data-fetching screens while async operations are in progress.
verify: manual: simulate slow network (Android emulator network throttle), confirm spinner visible on Dashboard, Transactions, Goals, Budget screens

acceptance_criteria.description: A reusable EmptyState component with title, subtitle, and optional CTA is used on Goals, Transactions, Budget, and Dashboard screens when no data exists.
verify: manual: fresh account with no data — confirm empty state appears on each of the four screens

acceptance_criteria.description: A reusable ErrorState component with message and retry button is used on all async screens when data fetching fails.
verify: manual: disconnect network, open Dashboard — confirm error state with retry button appears
manual: tap retry — confirm loading state then data or error reappears

acceptance_criteria.description: react-native-reanimated fade/slide transitions are applied between main tab screens.
verify: manual: navigate between tabs on device — smooth animated transition visible, no jank on mid-tier Android

acceptance_criteria.description: expo-haptics impact feedback fires on primary CTA presses (save budget, confirm OCR import, create goal) and on alert acknowledgements.
verify: manual: tap each CTA on physical Android device and confirm haptic feedback

acceptance_criteria.description: All interactive tap targets are at least 48 dp in both dimensions.
verify: manual: accessibility inspector (Android Layout Inspector or Expo) — verify tap target sizes

acceptance_criteria.description: Core actions (create budget, import receipt, create goal, view dashboard) remain reachable within 3 taps from any main screen.
verify: manual: tap count walkthrough for each core action from each bottom tab

acceptance_criteria.description: UI is visually validated on at least two Android screen sizes: compact (360dp) and medium (411dp).
verify: manual: run on Android emulator at 360dp and 411dp screen widths; verify no layout overflow or clipping

acceptance_criteria.description: GEMINI_API_KEY environment variable is configured in Azure Container Apps; key is never present in any source file or git history.
verify: manual: git log --all -S GEMINI_API_KEY (zero results)
manual: Azure Container Apps environment variables include GEMINI_API_KEY

acceptance_criteria.description: AI Chat tab is visible in mobile bottom navigation when AI_CHAT_ENABLED=true and hidden when false.
verify: manual: set AI_CHAT_ENABLED=false, confirm chat tab absent from navigation
manual: set AI_CHAT_ENABLED=true, confirm chat tab appears

acceptance_criteria.description: POST /api/v1/ai/chat system prompt includes current month budget summary, per-category spending totals, couple city (Belo Horizonte), and today's date.
verify: manual: inspect outbound Gemini API payload (debug log); confirm system prompt fields present
manual: ask assistant 'what is my food budget this month?' — response reflects actual budget data

acceptance_criteria.description: Chat assistant can suggest restaurants and activities in Belo Horizonte within the user's current remaining category budget.
verify: manual: ask 'where can we eat tonight under R$80?' and confirm response names BH venues with price context

acceptance_criteria.description: Chat history is client-session only; refreshing or reopening the app clears chat history and there is no server-side chat log.
verify: manual: send 5 messages, close and reopen app — chat history is empty
manual: database inspection confirms no chat_messages table or similar structure

acceptance_criteria.description: Per-couple rate limiting of 30 requests/hour is enforced on POST /api/v1/ai/chat; request 31 returns HTTP 429.
verify: cmd: integration test — send 31 requests within 1 hour window, confirm 31st returns 429

acceptance_criteria.description: When Gemini API returns quota error (429), mobile displays 'assistant unavailable, try again later' without crashing.
verify: manual: mock Gemini 429 response — confirm graceful mobile error message appears

acceptance_criteria.description: Responses to investment or legal advice requests include a redirect to consult a qualified professional.
verify: manual: send 'Should I invest in stocks?' — response includes disclaimer to consult a financial advisor

acceptance_criteria.description: GEMINI_MODEL environment variable controls the model ID; default value is set in app configuration.
verify: manual: confirm GEMINI_MODEL env var present with default value in app config
manual: override GEMINI_MODEL and confirm backend uses updated model ID without redeployment

acceptance_criteria.description: AI Chat feature is only built and integrated after all other V1.5 features (AC-101 through AC-140) are complete and verified.
verify: manual: confirm tasks.yaml shows all Area 1-4 tasks as done before any AI Chat tasks begin
